title: ostericum florenti
parts: Ostericum florenti has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine. The roots of Ostericum florenti contain sesquiterpenes, including costunolide and dehydrocostus lactone, which have anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves and flowers of the plant contain flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the flowers of Ostericum florenti contain volatile oils, including camphor and borneol, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects: Ostericum florenti can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues in some individuals. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant may lead to gastrointestinal problems, including diarrhea, nausea, and stomach cramps. In rare cases, improper use of Ostericum florenti can also cause dizziness, headaches, and an increased heart rate. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using the plant for medicinal purposes to avoid these potential side effects.
